title: Hieracium lissodermum
taxonomy: Hieracium lissodermum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Hieracium, commonly known as hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium contains over 1000 species of plants, many of which are native to the Northern Hemisphere. Hieracium lissodermum is typically found in mountainous regions with dry to moist soils. This species is thought to be endemic to certain regions, although further research is needed to confirm its distribution.

intro: Hieracium lissodermum, also known as a species of hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium lissodermum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium lissodermum is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusement to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
